concerns.text: FR-003 mandates a 'Latin Square counterbalancing design' for the survey. The tasks (T023) explicitly implement a 'between-subjects' design where users see only ONE salience level. There is no task to implement Latin Square logic or repeated-measures presentation, leaving FR-003 unimplemented.

FR-005 requires a 'repeated-measures ANOVA'. Task T032 implements a 'One-Way ANOVA (Between-Subjects)' and GLMM. No task exists to implement the specific repeated-measures ANOVA algorithm required by the spec.

FR-007 requires a validation step via 'a pre-test panel OR artifact detection metric'. Tasks T016 and T047 implement the artifact detection metric, but no task exists to implement or manage a 'pre-test panel' option, leaving the 'OR' clause unaddressed if the metric fails.

SC-003 requires measuring power against a 'medium effect size (Cohen's f ≈ 0.25)'. The plan assumes a 'small effect size (f=0.15)' for sample sizing. No task exists to adjust the power analysis logic (T034) to specifically target the medium effect size required by the success criterion.

Task T014 describes loading a 'pre-curated, human-validated list' from an external source. This contradicts FR-001 which requires the system to 'download and parse metadata... to load a curated... list'. The task outsources the core curation logic defined in the spec without a corresponding task to implement the curation pipeline.
